design_philosophy.core_loop: Select ship form (Front/Side/Rear offense), collect power-ups to upgrade weapons, navigate scrolling levels while managing form transformations and limited bombs, optional co-op ship combination mechanic
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Form-switching strategy vs. enemy positioning; balancing offensive coverage (front vs. side vs. rear) against incoming threats; maintaining power-up levels through damage avoidance
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Piloting a shapeshifting ancient aircraft descended from the lost civilization of Mu, wielding mystical power to defend Earth from revived Atlantean monsters alongside a co-pilot sibling
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Three mutually-exclusive offensive stances create asymmetric risk-reward gameplay; transformation mechanic forces constant tactical decisions rather than optimal loadout selection; co-op union system subordinates one player's weapon control, creating dynamic social negotiation

difficulty.lives_system: Adjustable via options screen; power-down penalties on hit reduce firepower before death
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression structure implied; anti-piracy measure loops back to level 1 if defeated after level 2 (suggests level-gated advancement)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High: difficulty, default lives, and controls fully adjustable in options menu; power-up acquisition and form selection allow skill-based risk management

progression.upgrade_system: Dual power-up tracks: normal power-ups (blue orbs, 3 levels) and special power-ups (red orbs, 3 levels) per ship form; levels carry across form transformations; bombs (max 9), speed adjustments (±5 levels); combination parts for temporary co-op fusion
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential level completion; no documented ship unlocks (early prototype featured player selection, removed in final)
replayability_hooks: Four ship variants to select at start
Adjustable difficulty and lives create varied challenge profiles
Sound test in options screen suggests mastery content
Co-op union mechanics create emergent asymmetric strategies

world_building_techniques: Static plot exposition: ancient Mu civilization, Atlantean monsters, 10,000-year cycle of conflict
Player character identity reveal: Asuka and Maya are descended from Mu warrior, not human siblings
Apocalyptic framing: modern weapons fail, parents sacrifice to awaken children to destiny
Archaeological parentage twist (adoptive reveal) provides emotional stakes for world-saving mission

boss_design.philosophy: Large-scale spectacle enabled by VRC4 custom chip; fire-breathing attack animations suggest elemental/mythological design
notable_bosses: Final boss features multi-stage fire-breathing animations (only front-facing animation used; side/rear variants remain unused, suggesting dev iteration on attack patterns)

faction_indicators: Modern humanity (helpless without Mu heritage activation)
danger_signals: Ominous shared dreams precede monster revival
Global military failure against Atlantean weapons
environmental_storytelling: Parallax scrolling suggests depth and scale of devastated world
Famicom hardware pushed to limits (flicker, sprite overflow noted in reviews) creates visual representation of system strain under Atlantean assault
VRC4 chip four-way scrolling enables environmental scope beyond typical Famicom shooters

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Crisis Force
